title: ‘Intuitive, Sure-Footed, and High Quality’ Define the Winners of Translation Prizes
summary:
This week, the winners of The Goethe-Institut New York Wolff and Gutekunst Prizes and the UK's Comma Press Emerging Translation Prize were announced. 

The post ‘Intuitive, Sure-Footed, and High Quality’ Define the Winners of Translation Prizes appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: Virginia Roberts Giuffre, Sarah Wynn-Williams Share Freedom to Publish Award at the 2026 British Book Awards
summary:
“Books are not monuments. They are instructions. Not merely to record what happened to one person. But to change the way the reader sees the world,” author Sarah Wynn-Williams said in her acceptance speech.

The post Virginia Roberts Giuffre, Sarah Wynn-Williams Share Freedom to Publish Award at the 2026 British Book Awards appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: Duke’s Allison Belan to Succeed Brian O’Leary at the Book Industry Study Group
summary:
Belan succeeds Brian O’Leary, who will retire from the role on June 30th.

The post Duke’s Allison Belan to Succeed Brian O’Leary at the Book Industry Study Group appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: Beijing International Book Fair Celebrates 40th Anniversary in June
summary:
Next month, the Beijing International Book Fair will welcome 1,700 exhibitors from 82 countries and regions to discuss the impact and innovation of AI, expansion and opportunity of global licensing, and the evolution of reading.

The post Beijing International Book Fair Celebrates 40th Anniversary in June appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: At the AAP’s Annual Meeting, Talk of AI, Copyright, and ‘Ripples of Hope’
summary:
The Association of American Publishers hosted its 2026 Annual Meeting on May 7 with a 90-minute virtual program that featured talks with Pulitzer Prize-winning historian and presidential biographer Jon Meacham and Stanford University copyright scholar and author Paul Goldstein.

The post At the AAP’s Annual Meeting, Talk of AI, Copyright, and ‘Ripples of Hope’ appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: New PEN America Report Finds Nonfiction Increasingly Targeted by Book Banners in U.S. Schools
summary:
“The content of these books that are disappearing speaks volumes about both the stories, identities, and facts that are being suppressed, and about the political and cultural attack on public education across the U.S.,” the report states.

The post New PEN America Report Finds Nonfiction Increasingly Targeted by Book Banners in U.S. Schools appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: Around the Book World: Monday, May 11, 2026
summary:
Publishing analyst Carlo Carrenho kicks off the week with a review of the headlines from across the international publishing world.

The post Around the Book World: Monday, May 11, 2026 appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: A Bookstore Grows in Damascus
summary:
Al Manhal Bakery and Books hopes to be a space where Syrians can come together to learn about their own history and discuss deep issues following 54 years of dictatorship and censorship.

The post A Bookstore Grows in Damascus appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: FoSci Releases Report Analyzing Growing Threats to Research Integrity
summary:
In Vancouver this week, the Forensic Scientometrics movement presented their first report, based on information from 17 experts in the field, highlighting the wide, often systemic, threats to scientific research.

The post FoSci Releases Report Analyzing Growing Threats to Research Integrity appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.

title: Despite a Thriving Market, U.K. Report Finds Comics Creators Are Struggling
summary:
While the comics sector is booming—the report cites NielsenIQ BookScan figures showing that the U.K. comics market reached its highest-ever total sales in 2025—creators say the professional landscape has become 'increasingly precarious.'

The post Despite a Thriving Market, U.K. Report Finds Comics Creators Are Struggling appeared first on Publishing Perspectives.
